summary: v2.5.6 Unknown-Doc Reclaim smart + skip_noise modes — full backend verification PASSED. 20/20 unit tests (test_unknown_doc_reclaim.py + test_unknown_doc_reclaim_smart.py) + 18/18 live HTTP integration tests (7 v2.5.5 carry-over + 11 new v2.5.6). All 9 review-request scenarios green. Combined total 38/38.
critical_code_review_comments: Noise precedence is correctly implemented in run(): `if skip_noise and _is_noise(...)` short-circuits BEFORE the smart-inheritance branch, so noise always wins over smart inheritance — matches the product requirement ('email sprites never belong in inbox').

NOISE_FILENAME_PATTERNS verified safe against the 5 real filenames flagged in the review request (W117505.pdf, Invoice-0493680.pdf, Gamer Ship 3626.pdf, MARCH 2026 ACTIVITY.pdf, 0303382.pdf) — none matched. Anchored with ^ in each pattern prevents mid-string false positives.
_is_noise() handles None/empty string safely (returns False) — no crashes on docs without file_name.
_build_reclaim_update returns (update, path_taken) tuple — cleanly separates the inherited vs plain counter increments in run(). Good refactor.

Smart inheritance correctly guards vendor_canonical + vendor_id + customer_canonical with `and not doc.get(...)` so existing child fields are never clobbered (verified by test_smart_mode_does_not_overwrite_existing_child_vendor).
Audit preservation: when smart-inherited, child's original (garbage) doc_type is kept under `doc_type_from_reclaim_ai` for future debugging — verified live.

Legacy `reclaimed_count` = plain + inherited (NOT including noise) — correct back-compat: noise docs never went to NeedsReview in v2.5.5 either since that field didn't exist. Downstream consumers of reclaimed_count see unchanged semantics.

Idempotency sentinel `reclaim_to_needs_review_at` is written by BOTH the reclaim-update AND the noise-update paths, so a second run correctly skips already-processed docs regardless of which path they took — verified live with test_idempotent_second_run_no_re_pick.
preview() mode counters correctly return None (not 0) when flags are OFF — this lets UI distinguish 'feature disabled' from 'zero candidates'. Verified.
Minor (non-blocking, pre-existing): router `limit: int = Query(None, ...)` on /run should ideally be `Optional[int]` for strict typing — works via FastAPI coercion.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_unknown_doc_reclaim_smart_http.py (NEW — 11 live HTTP tests: preview null-counters, preview flag ints + non-mutation, smart inheritance, smart no-parent plain path, skip_noise filters linkedin sprite, noise-wins-over-smart combined mode, idempotency snapshot-preservation, v2.5.5 default regression, default dry-run shape, dashboard + pattern-health regression)
test_credentials: admin/admin (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md — not needed; /api/admin/unknown-doc-reclaim/* endpoints are unauthenticated in preview env)
seed_data_creation: Per-test function-scoped fixtures seeded ephemeral TEST_iter228_* docs (parent + children: inh/plain/noise/np/idem variants) across 8 scenarios. All cleaned up at fixture teardown + module-scope safety-net delete_many({id: /^TEST_iter228_/}). Post-run verification: 0 leftover TEST_iter228_* docs in hub_documents and 0 TEST_iter228_* actor rows in unknown_doc_reclaim_runs. Real customer data untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: v2.5.6 smart + skip_noise reclaim modes fully verified on live preview env. Service module exports NOISE_FILENAME_PATTERNS + _is_noise for reuse. The /runs endpoint audit trail includes iter228 TEST_ rows (deleted during cleanup) + prior ci_test/ci_test_idem rows from iter227 — those prior v2.5.5 rows auto-picked up the new v2.5.6 counter fields as 0 (back-compat confirmed). mongomock-motor 0.0.36 + pymongo for live DB seeding both working. The 4 pre-existing failures in test_validation_auto_clear_readiness_fixes.py mentioned in the context were NOT re-run this iteration (unrelated). If production sweep is next, user-facing impact will be: 372 raw candidates → significant reduction after skip_noise filters email sprites/signatures, smart mode will inherit parent doc_type for the 31/50 batch children.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All 9 features/scenarios from the v2.5.6 review request verified green. One minor note: while authoring the HTTP test suite, initial implementation used module-scope seeding which caused test ordering contamination (smart-only run processed noise-filename docs before skip_noise tests could see them — by design, since smart alone doesn't filter noise). Refactored to function-scope per-test seeding for clean isolation; all 11 pass consistently now.
